fun_fact: India's cities are losing their green cover fast — Bengaluru's tree canopy has shrunk to under 3% even as it kept the name "Garden City," with similar declines across Mumbai, Delhi and Hyderabad. Urban trees and forests cool neighbourhoods, clean the air, shelter wildlife and give people a shared "third space" to gather — which is exactly why patches like Aarey, Cubbon, KBR and the Aravallis are worth protecting before the next flyover or road takes them.
